colors

colors <- paletteer::palettes_d_names
my_palette <- paletteer_d("ggsci::indigo_material")
my_palette
## <colors>
## #E8EAF6FF #C5CAE9FF #9FA8DAFF #7986CBFF #5C6BC0FF #3F51B5FF #3949ABFF #303F9FFF #283593FF #1A237EFF

#Adding variables

Separate date column

birds <- birds %>% separate(col="date", into = c("month","day","year"), sep = "/") %>% ##separate month into col
  select(!day) %>% ##remove day, since data already has a day col
  mutate(month=as.factor(month)) ##make month into a factor

Add season variable

birds <- birds %>% ##make a new column showing what season, since the data is split by spring or fall
  mutate(season=case_when(
    month=="4" | month=="5" | month=="6" ~ "spring",
    month=="8" | month=="9" | month=="10" | month=="11" ~ "fall",
    .default=NA
  ))

Adding migration level variable based on the paper

birds <- birds %>% 
  mutate(migration_level= case_when( ##migration level from the paper
    season=="spring" & amt>=4 & amt<=1860 ~ "medium", ##946 is average migration for spring
    season=="fall" & amt>=9 & amt<=3420 ~ "medium", ##1690 is avg for fall
    season=="spring" & amt<=4 ~ "low", ##avg-1SD
    season=="fall" & amt<=9 ~ "low",
  season=="spring" & amt>=1860 ~ "high", ##avg+1SD
    season=="fall" & amt>=3420 ~ "high"
  ))

Separate wind speed into low, med, high with same method as migration level: low<= avg-1SD, med<= avg+1SD, >= avg-1SD, high>=avg+1SD. I took the absolute value of the winds first, since negative wind is just the opposite direction of positive, and I want to compare strength, not direction

birds %>% 
  group_by(season) %>% 
  mutate(uwind_abs=abs(uwind), vwind_abs=abs(vwind)) %>% 
  summarise(mean(uwind_abs), sd(uwind_abs), mean(vwind_abs), sd(vwind_abs))
## # A tibble: 2 × 5
##   season `mean(uwind_abs)` `sd(uwind_abs)` `mean(vwind_abs)` `sd(vwind_abs)`
##   <chr>              <dbl>           <dbl>             <dbl>           <dbl>
## 1 fall                2.18            1.76              2.36            1.52
## 2 spring              2.46            2.13              2.65            1.59
birds <- birds %>% 
  mutate(uwind_abs=abs(uwind), vwind_abs=abs(vwind)) %>%
  mutate(uwind_level= case_when(
    season=="spring" & uwind_abs<= 2.457172-2.132746 ~"low",
    season=="fall" & uwind_abs<= 2.184764-1.760663 ~"low",
    season=="spring" & uwind_abs>= 2.457172+2.132746 ~"high",
    season=="fall" & uwind_abs>= 2.184764+1.760663 ~"high",
    season=="spring" & uwind_abs>= 2.457172-2.132746 & uwind<= 2.457172+2.132746 ~"medium",
    season=="fall" & uwind_abs>= 2.184764-1.760663 & uwind_abs<= 2.184764+1.760663 ~"medium",
  )) %>% 
  mutate(vwind_level= case_when(
    season=="spring" & vwind_abs<= 2.648716-1.587146 ~"low",
    season=="fall" & vwind_abs<= 2.357720-1.516771 ~"low",
    season=="spring" & vwind_abs>= 2.648716+1.587146 ~"high",
    season=="fall" & vwind_abs>= 2.357720+1.516771 ~"high",
    season=="spring" & vwind_abs>= 2.648716-1.587146 & vwind<= 2.648716+1.587146 ~"medium",
    season=="fall" & vwind_abs>= 2.357720-1.516771 & vwind_abs<= 2.357720+1.516771 ~"medium",
  ))

wind direction variable: positive vwind values indicating winds coming from the south, positive uwind values indicating winds coming from the west

birds <- birds %>% 
  mutate(uwind_direction= case_when(
    uwind>0 ~"west to east",
    uwind<0 ~"east to west", 
    uwind==0 ~"still",
  )) %>% 
  mutate(vwind_direction= case_when(
    vwind>0 ~"south to north",
    vwind<0 ~"north to south", 
    vwind==0 ~"still",

  ))
##  [1] "month"                 "year"                  "yday"                 
##  [4] "building"              "volunteer"             "count"                
##  [7] "uwind"                 "vwind"                 "ceiling_height"       
## [10] "visibility_distance"   "visibility"            "temperature"          
## [13] "amt"                   "scaled_yday"           "scaled_uwind"         
## [16] "scaled_vwind"          "scaled_ceiling_height" "scaled_temperature"   
## [19] "scaled_amt"            "season"                "migration_level"      
## [22] "uwind_abs"             "vwind_abs"             "uwind_level"          
## [25] "vwind_level"           "uwind_direction"       "vwind_direction"
